questions.section: Module II Reasoning and General Intelligence
questions.question_text_full:
In this question, two statements are followed by four conclusions given as options. Decide which conclusion is definitely true, based on the given statements.
Statements:
A < B ≥ C > D
C > E ≥ F = G

questions.section: Module II Reasoning and General Intelligence
questions.question_text_full:
A question is given, followed by two statements labelled I and II. Identify which of the statements is/are sufficient/necessary to answer the question.
Question:
In a certain code language, ‘fire in mountain’ is coded as ‘si ph bo’. What is code for ‘fire’?
Statements:
(I) ‘mountain’ is coded as ‘bo’.
(II) ‘ice and fire’ is coded as ‘si yu ca’.

options.text: Data in Statements I and II together is sufficient to answer the question
Data in Statements I and II together is not sufficient to answer the question
Data in Statement I alone is sufficient to answer the question while data in Statement II is not
Data in Statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question while data in Statement I is not

questions.correct_option_text: Data in Statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question while data in Statement I is not

questions.question_text_full: Select the option figure in which the given figure is embedded as its part (rotation is NOT allowed).

Given below is a statement (Cause) followed by possible effects numbered I, II and III. Read the ‘Cause’ carefully and decide which of the three can be possible effect/s.

Cause – Many people in City X have reported receiving two electricity bills this month, instead of one.

Effect I – The Electricity Board has published the details of areas where power shutdowns are expected tomorrow in City X.
Effect II – The municipal commissioner of City X has clarified that the technical glitch which led to multiple electricity bills being generated would be resolved within 48 hours.
Effect III – Long queues were seen outside the electricity board offices in many localities.

options.text: Only II is a possible effect.
Both I and II are possible effects .
Only I is a possible effect.
Both II and III are possible effects.

questions.correct_option_text: Both II and III are possible effects.

questions.question_text_full: In a certain code language, ‘POSE’ is coded as ‘3517’ and ‘SILK’ is coded as ‘4638’. What is the code for ‘S’ in the given code language?

[Image of Y57nBe2 with mirror line MN]

Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets.
(NOTE : Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding / subtracting / multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)
(215, 185, 135)
(276, 246, 196)

How many meaningful English words can be formed using first, third, sixth and seventh letters of the word RECOGNIZE (when counted from left to right) using all the letters but each letter only once in each word?

The following contains two pairs of words which are related to each other in a certain way. Three of the following four word-pairs are alike as these have the same relationship and thus form a group. Which word-pair does NOT belong to that group?
(The words must be considered as meaningful English words and must not be grouped based on the number of letters/number of consonants/vowels in the word)

Read the following information and answer the question which follows.

"Similar to expert puzzle solvers, Solver S employs systematic problem-solving approaches, ensuring efficient and accurate resolutions to complex challenges."

Which of the following can be inferred from the given statement? (An inference is something which is not directly stated but can be derived from the given facts/information in the statement)
1. The efficiency and accuracy of Solver S in resolving complex challenges may be attributed to their systematic problem-solving methods.
2. Solver S utilizes systematic problem-solving approaches, similar to expert puzzle solvers.
U is the father of N. N is the sister of T. T is the wife of I. I is son of L. How is U related to L?

Select the correct option that indicates the arrangement of the following words in a logical and meaningful order.
1. Design 2. Test 3. Conceptualize 4. Develop 5. Implement

Select the correct option that indicates the arrangement of the following words in a logical and meaningful order.
1. Delivery
2. Finished Paper
3. Dry
4. Pulp
5. Press

Select the number from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series.
9, 5, 6, 10.5, 23, ?

Each of P, Q, R, S, T, U and V has an exam on a different day of a week, starting from Monday and ending on Sunday of the same week.
Q has the exam on Saturday. Only two people have exams between S and Q. R has the exam before S, but after P. U has the exam immediately after T.
On which of the following days does V have the exam?

In a certain code language,
A + B means ‘A is the mother of B’,
A – B means ‘A is the brother of B’,
A x B means ‘A is the wife of B’ and
A ÷ B means ‘A is the father of B’.
Based on the above, how is U related to Z if ‘U + V ÷ W – Y x Z’?

Four of the following five figures are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does NOT belong to that group?
[Figure A, B, C, D, E]
questions.notes: The question asks to identify the figure that does not belong. The options provided in the answer block are C, D, B, A.

questions.question_text_full: Identify the figure given in the options which when put in place of the question mark (?) will logically complete the series.
questions.notes: The correct option is marked with a green tick.

questions.question_text_full:
In the following number-pairs, the second number is obtained by applying certain mathematical operations to the first number. In three of the four pairs, the same pattern is applied and hence they form a group. Select the number-pair that does NOT belong to this group.
(NOTE : Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding /subtracting /multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)

A question is given, followed by two statements labelled I and II. Identify which of the statements is/are sufficient/necessary to answer the question.
Question:
Arvind has blue, green and white marbles. What is the percentage of white marbles out of all the marbles he has?
Statements:
(I) The sum of blue and green marbles he has is 7.
(II) He has 3 white marbles out of 10 marbles.

options.text: Data in Statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question while data in Statement I is not
Data in Statement I alone is sufficient to answer the question while data in Statement II is not
Data in Statements I and II together is sufficient to answer the question
Data in Statements I and II together is not sufficient to answer the question

questions.correct_option_text: Data in Statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question while data in Statement I is not

questions.question_text_full: Six letters P, Q, S, T, U and V are written on different faces of a dice. Two positions of this dice are shown in thefigure. Find the letter on the face opposite to T.

If 'A' stands for '÷', 'B' stands for 'x', 'C' stands for '+' and 'D' stands for '-', what will be come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation?

55 B 5 D 13 B 2 C 14 A 7 D 15 B 5 C 102 A 2 C 15 = ?
Each of the digits in the number 5238641 is arranged in ascending order from left to right. The position(s) of how many digits will remain unchanged as compared to that/those in the original number ?

A statement is followed by two arguments I and II. Read the statement and the arguments carefully and select the appropriate answer from the given options.

Statement:
Hydroelectricity is a cleaner and greener energy than other forms of power generation.

Arguments:
(I) Hydroelectric plants almost have nil CO2 emissions.
(II) Hydroelectric power projects submerge huge amounts under water thereby affecting local flora and fauna.

options.text: Both I and II weaken the statement.
II weakens while I strengthens the statement.
Both I and II strengthen the statement.
I weakens while II strengthens the statement.

questions.correct_option_text: II weakens while I strengthens the statement.

# : XAF :: SVA : %

Q is not an immediate neighbour of P. O sits second to the right of S. Which of the following represents the position of O with respect of R?

questions.question_text_full:
Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statement(s) is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s).

Statements:
All sheeps are cows.
Some cows are horses.
All horses are jackals.

Conclusion:
(I) Some jackals are cows.
(II) Some horses are sheeps.

questions.section: Module I English Language and Comprehension
questions.question_text_full:
Select the correct spelling of the incorrectly spelt word in the given sentence.

The trainer tried to guage the learners' understanding of the subject by asking several new questions.

Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined word.
He walked leisurely towards the entrance.

Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph.
A. It consists of a terracotta army of approximately 8000 life-sized men and horses.
B. Each of the horses and men is individually sculpted by a 3-inch-thick terracotta clay and looks unique.
C. Shi Huangdi was the founder of Qin Dynasty and was the first ruler of unified China in 221 BC.
D. His mausoleum was recently discovered in 1974 by Chinese peasants who were drilling a well.

Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Select the option that gives their correct logical sequence to form a meaningful sentence.
a. it requires constant sustenance
b. in order for life to be liveable,
c. that enable it to grow and develop
d. and favourable circumstances

Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.
No other book in the series captivated readers as ________________ as the final installment.

A sentence has been split up into segments and given below in jumbled order. While the first and the last segments of the sentence (1 and 6) are in the correct order, the segments in between are jumbled up. Arrange the segments in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent sentence.
1) India has been a land
P) and the learned man has been held in higher esteem
Q) of learning through the ages
R) but in the sense that learning has always been very highly valued
S) not indeed in the sense that education has been universal
6) than the warrior or the administrator.

Select the option that expresses the given sentence in reported speech.
'I am determined to publish my findings,' the scientist asserted confidently.

options.text: The scientist confidently asserted that she is determined to publish her findings.
The scientist confidently asserted that she had determined to publish her findings.
The scientist confidently asserted that she would be determined to publish her findings.
The scientist confidently asserted that she was determined to publish her findings.

questions.correct_option_text: The scientist confidently asserted that she was determined to publish her findings.

questions.question_text_full:
Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.
One who cannot make a mistake

Select the most appropriate homophone from the options to fill in the blank.
We should _________ away all prejudices to live in harmony.

Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph.
A. As cost of raw materials cannot be reduced, exporters try to cut labour costs.
B. Large MNCs in the garment industry in Europe and America order their products from Indian exporters.
C. These large MNCs with worldwide network look for the cheapest goods in order to maximise their profits.
D. To get these large orders, Indian garment exporters try hard to cut their own costs.
E. Where earlier a factory used to employ workers on a permanent basis, now they employ workers on a temporary basis so that they do not have to pay workers for the whole year.

Select the correct spelling of the incorrectly spelt word in the given sentence.
All criminal attitudes must be niped at the bud.

Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech.
The teacher informed that the exam had been postponed.

options.text: The teacher informs, “The exam had been postponed.”
The teacher informed, “The exam was being postponed.”
The teacher informs, “The exam has been postponed.”
The teacher informed, “The exam has been postponed.”

questions.correct_option_text: The teacher informed, “The exam has been postponed.”

questions.question_text_full:
Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph.
A. Psychotherapy is a voluntary relationship between the one seeking treatment or the client and the one who treats or the therapist.
B. The relationship is conducive for building the trust of the client so that problems may be freely discussed.
C. The purpose of the relationship is to help the client to solve the psychological problems being faced by her or him.
D. Psychotherapies aim at changing the maladaptive behaviours, decreasing the sense of personal distress and helping the client to adapt better to her/his environment.
E. Inadequate marital, occupational and social adjustment also requires that major changes be made in an individual's personal environment.

Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.
Rashid was not able to _____ the pain after the accident.

Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.
Did you hear that noise?

options.text: Was that noise heard by you?
Is that noise heard by you?
Wasn't that noise heard by you?
Has that noise been heard by you?

questions.correct_option_text: Was that noise heard by you?

questions.question_text_full:
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech.
The District Magistrate ordered the police to work all night and put an end to those riots.

options.text: The District Magistrate told the police, "Work all night and put to end these riots."
The District Magistrate told the police, "Work all night and put an end to these riots."
The District Magistrate told the police, "Work all night and put an end to their riots."
The District Magistrate told the police, "Work all night to put an end these riots."

questions.correct_option_text: The District Magistrate told the police, "Work all night and put an end to these riots."

questions.question_text_full:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom.
The team decided to pull out all the stops for their upcoming presentation.

options.text: Wait patiently for a more favourable time
Give up easily and without thinking much
To do everything to make something successful

questions.correct_option_text: To do everything to make something successful

questions.question_text_full:
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.
Everyone was surprised at his speech.

options.text: His speech was surprising to everyone.

questions.question_text_full:
Complete the dialogue for Person B using the correct idiom.
Person A: I am so nervous. I don't think I should make the speech tomorrow.
Person B: No. You are just getting ______. You have prepared for this.

own efforts for success.

the correct order to form a meaningful sentence.

a. beginning in the 19th century,

b. was long a mystery, and

c. the origin of the Moon

d. three competing theories emerged to solve it

Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.
The mango is the ________ fruit.

Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.
The explosion caused serious damage in Ukraine.

options.text: In Ukraine, serious damage had been caused by the explosion.
Serious damage has been caused by the explosion in Ukraine.
Serious damage in Ukraine has been caused by the explosion.
Serious damage was caused by the explosion in Ukraine.

questions.correct_option_text: Serious damage was caused by the explosion in Ukraine.

Enhance

A sudden and violent change or upheaval

Generous

It takes me an hour to get to work at the moment, but when the new road is finished it be much quicker.

options.text: but when the new road is finished
It takes me an hour to get to work

Select the most appropriate pronoun to fill in the blank.
Mr. Amit Kumar, Proprietor of Xyling Pens is known for __________ honesty and decent behaviour in the business arena.

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.
Accumulated

questions.question_text_full: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.

What was the primary reason for the Japanese to support Subhas Chandra Bose and the formation of the INA?

options.text: They believed in India's right to self-determination.
They were looking for allies in their fight against the British Empire.
They believed in Bose's vision of a communist India.
They wanted to expand their influence in Southeast Asia.

questions.correct_option_text: They wanted to expand their influence in Southeast Asia.

questions.question_text_full: What ultimately undermined the INA's efforts to free India from British rule?

options.text: The inferior military tactics of the British forces
The lack of support from the Indian and British people

questions.question_text_full: What was the significance of Bose’s slogan ‘Jai Hind’?

options.text: It symbolised the struggle against British colonialism.
It was a call to arms for the Indian and British Army.
It manifests the vision of selected communities.
It represented Bose’s vision of a capitalist India.

questions.correct_option_text: It symbolised the struggle against British colonialism.

questions.question_text_full: How did Subhas Chandra Bose view India's fight for independence?
What does the term 'multiphyletic group' mean in the context of algae?

options.text: No common ancestor, not closely related

questions.correct_option_text: No common ancestor, not closely related

questions.question_text_full: Which statement accurately describes vascular plants based on the information in the passage?

options.text: Vascular plants lack stems, roots and leaves.
Vascular plants are unicellular in structure.
Vascular plants have true stems, roots and leaves, unlike algae.
Algae and vascular plants share the same morphoanatomical characteristics.

questions.correct_option_text: Vascular plants have true stems, roots and leaves, unlike algae.

questions.question_text_full: According to the passage, which is the main pigment in algae for photosynthesis?
What is the central theme of the passage?

options.text: The prokaryotic nature of blue-green algae
The symbiotic relationship between algae and fungi
The diversity and characteristics of algae

questions.correct_option_text: The diversity and characteristics of algae

questions.question_text_full: Select the most appropriate synonym of the word 'Inherent'.
What is the meaning of the phrase 'a clarion call' in the passage?

Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 2.

i. All isoclines are expansion paths but all expansion paths are not isoclines.
ii. Short-run production functions involve adjusting all inputs, including fixed inputs like land or capital, to affect output.
iii. Stock resources are the resources which are not used in one period of production but can be stored for a later period.
Which of the following is a monoatomic gas?

Select the most appropriate answer from the options given below regarding Census 2011.
Statement 1. The population of India has increased by more than 181 million during the decade 2001-2011.
Statement 2. The percentage decadal growth during 2001-2011 has registered the sharpest decline since Independence.

options.text: Both statements 1 and 2 are not correct.

questions.question_text_full: What is the name of the ion with a charge of -1?

In December 1991, which act was amended to bring public enterprises under the purview of the Board for Industrial and Financial Reconstruction (BIFR) as part of the country's economic reforms program?
Which theory, independently introduced in 1923, led to the concept of acid-base conjugate pairs?
Who mentioned ecosystem as 'the basic unit in ecology' in 1956?

options.text: Ministry of Ports Shipping and Waterways
Ministry of Environment and Forest and Climate Change
Ministry of Road Transport and Highways

questions.correct_option_text: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways

questions.question_text_full: Mathura was the second capital of _________ dynasty.

Which of the following statements is/are correct?
Statements:
1) Ampere's Law describes the relationship between electric current and the magnetic field it produces.
2) Maxwell's equations are a set of four fundamental equations governing electric and magnetic fields in the absence of charges and currents.
Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease is caused by duplication of the peripheral myelin protein-22 (PMP22) gene on which chromosome?

Which portal allowed stakeholders seamless access to information related to funding, documentation, project monitoring, and approval through four efficient ways of logging in for monitoring the implementation of Centrally Sponsored Schemes in 2023?

options.text: Pradhan Mantri Jan bhagidari Yojana Portal

questions.question_text_full: Who among the following was the first Home Minister of Independent India?
What was the status of the agricultural sector in India before the Green Revolution?

options.text: Use of old technology and the absence of required infrastructure
Use of advanced technology and high productivity
Use of advanced technology and dependency on rainfall
Use of traditional technology and the abundance of required infrastructure

questions.correct_option_text: Use of old technology and the absence of required infrastructure

questions.question_text_full: In which year did Walter Fleming stain the chromosomes to see them clearly and describe the entire process of mitosis?

List 1 List 2
A. The Philosophy of the Bomb 1. Sachindranath Sanyal
B. Bandi Jiwan 2. Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay
C. Pather Dabi 3. Bhagwati Charan Vohra
D. Anandmath 4. Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay
The rotating columns of air that occur over water bodies and are generally less severe than land tornadoes are known as:
In which of the following cases did the Supreme Court direct that, 'the Parliament cannot take away or abridge any of the fundamental rights for the implementation of Directive Principles'?

Who urged political parties to engage in discussions that inspire unity rather than division and promote ideas instead of personal attacks in the address on the eve of National Voters Day in January 2024?
Who won the title at the World Chess Armageddon Asia & Oceania event in April 2023?

Select the option that is true regarding the following two statements labelled Assertion (A) and Reason (R).

A. The first battle of Panipat was one of the decisive battles of India.
R. It brought into power a new band of invaders called Mughals.

options.text: Statements A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
Statements A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
R is true, but A is false.
A is true, but R is false.

questions.correct_option_text: Statements A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.

questions.question_text_full: In the year 2020, National Digital Health Mission was launched on which of the following occasions?
Which Indian city experiences the LEAST difference in average temperature between its hottest and coldest months?
Which of the following technologies allows real-time communication over the Internet, such as voice and video calls, without requiring additional software or hardware beyond a web browser?
What is the primary advantage of being able to insert and delete slides in an MS PowerPoint presentation?

options.text: To enable the addition of multimedia content
To allow for dynamic content adjustment and organisation
To apply different templates to individual slides

questions.correct_option_text: To allow for dynamic content adjustment and organisation

questions.question_text_full: Which method is used to protect sensitive information during data transmission?
Which is the correct keyboard shortcut for adjusting the column width in MS Excel?
Which action should be taken in Microsoft Outlook to send a received email to another person, while keeping the original message for your records?

options.text: Use the 'Archive' option and create a new email.
Open the email, click on 'Forward', and enter the recipient's email address.
Click on 'Reply' and add the new recipient's email address.
Click on 'Reply All' and select 'Forward' from the options.

questions.correct_option_text: Open the email, click on 'Forward', and enter the recipient's email address.

questions.question_text_full: What security mechanism ensures that system updates are installed without user intervention?
Which keyboard shortcut is used for adding bullets in MS Word 2010?
Which device is commonly used to connect computers within a LAN?
What is the primary reason DRAM needs to be refreshed periodically?

options.text: To prevent data corruption caused by magnetic fields
To recharge the capacitors storing data bits
To allow the CPU to access new data

questions.correct_option_text: To recharge the capacitors storing data bits

questions.question_text_full: Which type of virus infects the boot sector of floppy disks or the Master Boot Record (MBR) of hard disks?
Which feature in MS Word allows the user to apply a consistent look across text elements such as headings, titles and subtitles?
What is the main purpose of using a template when creating a presentation in MS PowerPoint?

options.text: To create interactive quizzes within slides

questions.question_text_full: Which of the following best describes Electronic Mail (E-mail)?

options.text: A method of sending messages electronically over a computer network.
A system for storing digital files on the cloud.
A type of secure online transaction system.
A service used to make phone calls over the internet.

questions.correct_option_text: A method of sending messages electronically over a computer network.

questions.question_text_full:
Match the Task Manager tabs in column A with their respective functionalities in column B.
Column A Column B
1. Processes a) Monitors overall CPU, memory, disk, and network usage
2. Performance b) Displays active applications and background processes
3. Startup c) Manages programs that run during system startup
4. App History d) Shows resource usage for Windows Store apps
Which operation can one directly perform using File Explorer in Windows?

Which of the following statements about computer memory is/are correct?
RAM is volatile memory, meaning it loses data when power is turned off.
ROM is used to store essential instructions required for the computer to boot.

Select which of the given statement(s) is/are True or False for the Central Processing Unit (CPU) of the Computer Systems.
(i) The CPU's clock speed controls the timing and execution speed of instructions.
(ii) The CPU's cache memory provides high-speed access to frequently used instructions and data.

questions.question_text_full: Which of the following is an example of a type of ROM that can be electronically erased and reprogrammed multiple times?

Column A
1. Search Bar
2. Quick Access
3. Wildcards
4. File Sorting
Column B
a) Displays files and folders recently accessed
b) Allows grouping and sorting of files by attributes
c) Refines searches for files with partial names
d) Locates files and folders by typing keywords
